1.gl: properly, something inherited, i.e. (abstractly) occupancy, or (concretely) an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion
1.kj: heritage, to inherit, inheritance, possession. Compare H5158 (נַחַל).

1.gl: a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also (figuratively) a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts
1.dv: rarely (shortened) גֹּי; apparently from the same root as H1465 (גֵּוָה) (in the sense of massing);

2.gl: to give, used with greatest latitude of application (put, make, etc.)
2.kj: add, apply, appoint, ascribe, assign, [idiom] avenge, [idiom] be (healed), bestow, bring (forth, hither), cast, cause, charge, come, commit, consider, count, [phrase] cry, deliver

2.gl: alive; hence, raw (flesh); fresh (plant, water, year), strong; also (as noun, especially in the feminine singular and masculine plural) life (or living thing), whether literally or figuratively
2.kj: [phrase] age, alive, appetite, (wild) beast, company, congregation, life(-time), live(-ly), living (creature, thing), maintenance, [phrase] merry, multitude, [phrase] (be) old, qui

2.gl: the sky (as aloft; the dual perhaps alluding to the visible arch in which the clouds move, as well as to the higher ether where the celestial bodies revolve)
2.dv: dual of an unused singular שָׁמֶה; from an unused root meaning to be lofty;

2.gl: a flabby thing, i.e. a carcase or carrion (human or bestial, often collectively); figuratively, an idol
2.kj: (dead) body, (dead) carcase, dead of itself, which died, (beast) that (which) dieth of itself.

2.gl: flesh (from its freshness); by extension, body, person; also (by euphemistically) the pudenda of aman
2.kj: body, (fat, lean) flesh(-ed), kin, (man-) kind, [phrase] nakedness, self, skin.

2.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
2.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
2.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

3.gl: a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle
3.kj: else, except, fail, (father-) less, be gone, in(-curable), neither, never, no (where), none, nor, (any, thing), not, nothing, to nought, past, un(-searchable), well-nigh, without.
3.dv: as if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist;

3.gl: blood (as that which when shed causes death) of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively (especially in the plural) bloodshed (i.e. drops of blood)
to spill forth (blood, a libation, liquid metal; or even a solid, i.e. to mound up); also (figuratively) to expend (life, soul, complaint, money, etc.); intensively, to sprawl out
3.kj: cast (up), gush out, pour (out), shed(-der, out), slip.

3.gl: (as noun) a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly (as adverb, with or without preposition) around
3.kj: (place, round) about, circuit, compass, on every side.

3.gl: water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen
3.dv: dual of a primitive noun (but used in a singular sense);

4.gl: a resident; by extension, a fellow-citizen

5.kj: alway(-s), constantly, end, ([phrase] n-) ever(more), perpetual, strength, victory.

5.gl: to kindle, i.e. consume (by fire or by eating);  to be(-come) brutish
5.kj: be brutish, bring (put, take) away, burn, (cause to) eat (up), feed, heat, kindle, set (on fire), waste.

5.gl: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
5.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

6.gl: an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character

7.kj: comely, dwelling (place), fold, habitation, pleasant place, sheepcote, stable, tarried.

8.gl: to slacken or be feeble; figuratively, to be oppressed
8.kj: bring low, dry up, be emptied, be not equal, fail, be impoverished, be made thin.

or חֵק; and חוֹק; from an unused root, apparently meaning to inclose;

13.dv: a primitive root; used only as denominative from H3027 (יָד); literally, to use (i.e. hold out) the hand;

13.gl: properly, to score with a mark as a tally or record, i.e. (by implication) to inscribe, and also to enumerate; intensively, to recount, i.e. celebrate
13.kj: commune, (ac-) count; declare, number, [phrase] penknife, reckon, scribe, shew forth, speak, talk, tell (out), writer.

13.gl: a collective name for a flock (of sheep or goats); also figuratively (of men)
13.dv: or צאוֹן; (Psalm 144:13), from an unused root meaning to migrate;

13.gl: a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of Israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock
properly, a revolution of time, i.e. an age or generation; also a dwelling
